hook.text: You wait in line, fill a form, and get told to come back tomorrow. Bureaucracy frustrates everyone. It persists.

buildup.text: Weber defined bureaucracy as a rational system: hierarchy, rules, specialization, and impersonal authority.

discovery.text: Bureaucracy replaced patronage and nepotism. Decisions follow rules, not personal connections. That's the theory.

twist.text: In practice, bureaucracies become rigid, slow, and self-serving. The rules meant to ensure fairness can entrench it.

climax.text: Weber called it the iron cage: efficient systems that trap people in dehumanizing routines they can't escape.

punchline.text: Bureaucracy was built to be fair. It became a cage instead.

quiz.question: What did Weber call the iron cage?
options: Efficient systems that trap people in dehumanizing routines
